title: Maybe we are just giving it too much thought
display_title: Maybe we are just giving it too much thought
wikitext:
[]: Maybe we are just giving it too much thought.....             Quote Post by Dima » Mon Nov 12, 2012 6:33 am Have you ever thought that we are giving the Eva universe much thought?I mean we all know that the Eva universe is huge,with many details,mysteries,personalities,things to analyse but some times i think we are speculating things that not even the creators have thought about them.... There are hundreds of topics discussing many details that are found on the TV serie or movies,topics that were created just from speculation or a word of from just an image..... I too myself have speculated many things regarding the EVA series but lately i've been thinking ''maybe we are just giving it too much thought''.....

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Mon Nov 12, 2012 6:41 am Is this for real?

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Mon Nov 12, 2012 7:00 am Blue Monday wrote: Is this for real?

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Mon Nov 12, 2012 9:00 am Have I ever thought it? Yes. Do I want to believe it? Hell no, I have enough trouble forgiving an author when they tell me they didn't have the major plot points planned out. Though I do think people over think some things.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Nov 12, 2012 9:52 am I find myself using the "it's fiction" or "it's a cartoon" defense a lot, so yeah, I do think it get's over-thought sometimes.

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Mon Nov 12, 2012 10:17 am Glad i wasnt the only one thinking about it! For a minute i thought i was just getting old.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Nov 12, 2012 10:59 am Dima wrote: For a minute i thought i was just getting old.

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Mon Nov 12, 2012 11:13 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Just wait until you're pushing 30.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Mon Nov 12, 2012 12:06 pm You are (not) alone. I often think, that people here are thinking about ridiculous details.

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Mon Nov 12, 2012 12:19 pm I would argue it's just the opposite. Anno and Co. spent years working on this project. I guarantee their thoughts were consumed with the story, its universe, characters, etc. prior and throughout the duration of the series, all day, every day. The 26 episodes and movies we got out of it are infinitesimal compared to the amount of thought they put into creating it. There are in innumerable amount of allusions and subtexts that, to this day, we can't decipher or haven't even noticed. I hate when people think that Gainex just threw together something without much thought. They clearly intricately constructed this universe that we still discuss and debate nearly 20 years after its inception.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Mon Nov 12, 2012 12:29 pm I think most of as are aware but giving too much thoughts is often where the fun lies.

[]:             Quote Post by Dima » Mon Nov 12, 2012 12:50 pm Merkaba wrote: I would argue it's just the opposite. Anno and Co. spent years working on this project. I guarantee their thoughts were consumed with the story, its universe, characters, etc. prior and throughout the duration of the series, all day, every day. The 26 episodes and movies we got out of it are infinitesimal compared to the amount of thought they put into creating it. There are in innumerable amount of allusions and subtexts that, to this day, we can't decipher or haven't even noticed. I hate when people think that Gainex just threw together something without much thought. They clearly intricately constructed this universe that we still discuss and debate nearly 20 years after its inception.

[]:             Quote Post by Merkaba » Mon Nov 12, 2012 12:58 pm But who is to say what people speculate is wrong? That is, what if whatever "crazy" theories people have that may or may not have even crossed the creators' minds DID, in fact, cross their minds? We'll never know. That's my point, I suppose. That so much brain power went into the project, anyone would be hard-pressed to say with absolute certainty that the creators didn't give [fill in the blank] meaning.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Nov 12, 2012 2:18 pm Contemplating one's entertainment is just another way to keep the mind active, creative, and honed. It's not for everyone, but I'd say it only deserves to be slammed ("you're thinking too hard!", and the like) when it actually detracts from quality of life in some way. (And even in those cases of hyperzealous nerdery, it's probably more the case that a person has a preexisting problem that they "medicate" with obsessive fandom, rather than the fandom being the intrinsic cause.)

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Nov 12, 2012 2:44 pm The series is one where the main story sinks hooks in; but it is maddeningly close to the edge of coherence. So when you're hooked by it, there's the urge to try and make it make sense. Now at times, this can get to levels of seeing faces in clouds (or glue on the cels ), but once you've started, it's fun to carry on.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Nov 12, 2012 2:46 pm This thread is 17 years too late

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Mon Nov 12, 2012 2:52 pm Yes, we are.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Nov 12, 2012 3:05 pm chee wrote: Yes, we are.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Mon Nov 12, 2012 3:59 pm I say we're not thinking hard enough! I mean, did you guys ever notice that the beginning of Rebuild sort of matches the End of-*assassinated*

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Mon Nov 12, 2012 4:36 pm Xard wrote: but you are hardly even post anymore!
